doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_buildopt_tls_p: build-time predicate reporting whether libmpfr was compiled with thread-safe / thread-local-storage support (MPFR_USE_THREAD_SAFE). C returns int (0 or 1) per a preprocessor branch; TS returns boolean.

The TS port returns the compile-time constant `false`. Pure-TS modules execute in a single-threaded JavaScript event loop -- there is no concept of cross-thread global state, no need for thread-local storage. Web Workers / Node Worker threads have their OWN module-level state by construction (each worker is a fresh isolate), so 'thread-local storage' is vacuously true at the worker boundary but the question 'does this libmpfr have TLS' has no meaningful TS analogue: there is no shared mutable __gmpfr_flags across workers in our port.

Returning `false` is the most honest answer: the TS port has no TLS infrastructure because it has no shared mutable state that would NEED TLS. This matches the convention used by the other buildopt_* ports in the batch (buildopt_float128_p, buildopt_decimal_p, etc.) which all return `false` for capabilities that don't translate to pure-TS.

Class 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget). Tag minimums (happy>=20, edge>=30, adversarial>=10, fuzz>=50, mined>=5) are inapplicable to no-arg accessor functions whose input domain is empty -- a single happy case is the entire test surface. This is the same carve-out used by mpfr_buildopt_float128_p; see worklog 016 / 017 for the precedent.
divergence_from_c: C returns int (0 or 1) from a preprocessor branch on MPFR_USE_THREAD_SAFE; TS returns boolean and is unconditionally `false`. Pure-TS has no shared cross-thread mutable state (each Worker is its own isolate), so the question 'does this libmpfr have TLS' has no meaningful answer in TS. Returning `false` is consistent with the rest of the buildopt_*_p ports in src/ops/. The golden_driver does NOT call libmpfr's mpfr_buildopt_tls_p() for the expected output (its return depends on how the system libmpfr was built); it hard-codes `false` to match the TS-port contract.
refs: mpfr/src/buildopt.c L25-L33 -- the C reference (mpfr_buildopt_tls_p preprocessor branch).
src/ops/buildopt_float128_p.ts -- sibling no-arg predicate template (returns false).
CLAUDE.md Rule 7 -- tag minimums (carved out here per the spec.doc note).
CLAUDE.md Rule 12 -- pure-TS port runs on Bun OR Node; both are single-threaded with worker isolates.
